close_reason: All 6 functions implemented: savePreKey (upsert), getPreKey, saveOTKs (batch), consumeOTK (atomic tx), getOTKCount, getKeyBundle. Added seedDevice factory. 97/97 tests green.
description:
Implement pre-key and one-time-key storage in src/keys/index.ts.

Privacy model: keys are NaCl Ed25519 public keys. Signature verification is route-level. Service functions only validate format and perform DB operations.

Functions to implement:

savePreKey(db, userID: string, deviceID: string, publicKey: string, signature: string, index: number): Promise<void>
  - Validates publicKey: /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/ (32-byte NaCl public key, hex-encoded)
  - Upserts: replaces existing preKey for that deviceID (deviceID is UNIQUE in preKeys table)
  - keyID = uuid.v4()

getPreKeys(db, deviceID: string): Promise<PreKey | null>
  - Returns the single preKey row for this device, or null

saveOTK(db, userID: string, deviceID: string, otks: OTKPayload[]): Promise<void>
  - Inserts multiple one-time keys; each keyID = uuid.v4()
  - Validates each publicKey format

getOTK(db, deviceID: string): Promise<OneTimeKey | null>
  - Atomically: SELECT one OTK ordered by index ASC LIMIT 1, then DELETE it
  - Returns null if none remain

getOTKCount(db, deviceID: string): Promise<number>
  - Returns count of remaining OTKs for device

getKeyBundle(db, deviceID: string): Promise<KeyBundle | null>
  - Fetches device (for signKey), preKey, and one OTK (consumed)
  - Returns null if device not found
  - otk is null if no OTKs remain (valid state — caller must handle)
  - Return type: { signKey: string, preKey: PreKey, otk: OneTimeKey | null }

Zod schemas (src/keys/schemas.ts):
  - PreKeySchema, OneTimeKeySchema, KeyBundleSchema
  - PublicKeySchema: z.string().regex(/^[0-9a-f]{64}$/)
  - SaveOTKBodySchema: z.array(OTKPayloadSchema)
title: Implement cryptographic key management

close_reason: src/errors.ts created with ConflictError, ValidationError, NotFoundError, AuthError, ForbiddenError — all with statusCode properties
description:
Before implementing HTTP routes (vex-chat-ekb), we need domain-specific error classes so route handlers can map errors to correct HTTP status codes without inspecting raw error messages.

Minimum set needed:
- ConflictError (→ 409): duplicate username, duplicate signKey, duplicate resource
- ValidationError (→ 400/422): invalid input format (should be caught at route layer, but DB layer may also throw)
- NotFoundError (→ 404): resource lookup returning null
- AuthError (→ 401): invalid credentials, expired token
- ForbiddenError (→ 403): valid auth but insufficient permission

Pattern:
- Custom classes extend Error with a statusCode or type field
- Centralized Express error middleware maps them to HTTP responses
- Service functions throw domain errors; route handlers do not inspect message strings
- Raw Kysely/SQLite errors that aren't ConflictError become 500s

Location: src/errors.ts (single file, all error classes)

This is a prerequisite for clean route implementation — without it, routes must string-match on error.message to decide status codes.
title: Add custom error classes before routes are built

description:
Write vitest tests for device management before implementation.

Privacy model: device registration requires a NaCl-signed action token proving the client owns the device signing key. NaCl verification is route-level; service functions receive pre-verified data.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- createDevice(db, owner, payload) creates a device row
  payload: { signKey (hex), preKey (hex), preKeySignature (hex), preKeyIndex, deviceName }
  device is created with deleted=0, lastLogin=null initially
- createDevice rejects duplicate signKey (UNIQUE constraint)
- createDevice rejects signKey not matching /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/ (must be 64 hex chars)
- createDevice also inserts a preKey row atomically (device + preKey together)
- retrieveDevice(db, deviceID) returns device or null
- retrieveDevice returns null for deleted devices (soft delete)
- retrieveUserDeviceList(db, userID) returns only non-deleted devices
- deleteDevice(db, deviceID) sets deleted=1, does not hard-delete
- markDeviceLogin(db, deviceID) updates lastLogin timestamp
- retrieveDevice by signKey (hex string) also works (upstream supports UUID or signKey lookup)
title: [TEST] Device management tests

description:
Create the application entry points for apps/spire.

src/run.ts (entry point)
- FIRST LINE (before any other imports): import { extendZodWithOpenApi } from '@asteasolutions/zod-to-openapi'; extendZodWithOpenApi(z);
  This must happen before any domain schema file is imported, or .openapi() metadata will be missing from the generated spec.
- Import config from ./config.js (parsed + validated via Zod)
- Import and create Kysely DB instance
- Import and start the Express app (HTTP routes + Scalar docs)
- Import and start the WebSocket server
- Handle graceful shutdown on SIGTERM/SIGINT: close DB connection, close WebSocket server

src/index.ts (barrel exports)

- Export Database type and Kysely instance factory
- Export all public TypeScript types (User, Device, Mail, Server, Channel, Permission, etc.)
- Export generateOpenAPIDocument() — allows consumers to get the spec programmatically

package.json additions:
- 'start': 'node dist/run.js'
- 'build': 'pnpm generate:openapi && tsc -p tsconfig.build.json'
- 'dev': 'node --watch --import tsx/esm src/run.ts'
- Verify 'generate:openapi' and 'lint:openapi' scripts are present (added in vex-chat-l3q)
title: Set up run.ts entry point and index.ts barrel exports

description:
Wire up OpenAPI spec linting as a pre-commit gate using Spectral and Husky.

.spectral.yaml (in apps/spire/):
- extends: ['spectral:oas']
- Disable: info-contact (off)
- Downgrade to warn: operation-description, oas3-parameter-description, tag-description
- Custom rule: assert no x-internal endpoints appear in public spec paths

Husky setup (workspace root):
- Install husky + lint-staged at workspace root (devDependencies)
- npx husky init → creates .husky/pre-commit
- .husky/pre-commit runs: pnpm --filter @vex-chat/spire lint-staged

lint-staged config (apps/spire/.lintstagedrc.json):
{
  'openapi.json': ['spectral lint --fail-severity=error --ruleset .spectral.yaml']
}

This means Spectral only runs when openapi.json is staged — no overhead on unrelated commits.

package.json verification:
- 'lint:openapi': 'spectral lint openapi.json --ruleset .spectral.yaml' is present (added in vex-chat-l3q)

CI drift check (.github/workflows/check-openapi.yml):
- On push/PR when src/** or openapi.json changes
- Step 1: pnpm generate:openapi
- Step 2: git diff --exit-code openapi.json — fails if committed spec doesn't match freshly generated one
- Step 3: spectral lint openapi.json --fail-severity=error
title: Set up Spectral + Husky pre-commit linting for openapi.json

close_reason: saveMail (insert), retrieveMail (SELECT+DELETE in transaction — relay model), deleteMail (no-op safe). 120/120 green (dcf2e94).
description:
Implement encrypted mail storage and retrieval in src/mail/.

Functions to implement:
- saveMail(db, mail: NewMail): Promise<Mail>
- retrieveMail(db, deviceID: string): Promise<Mail[]>
  IMPORTANT: retrieveMail must atomically delete the returned rows after reading them.
  The server is a relay, not a store. Mail is deleted from the server after the recipient fetches it.
  Implementation pattern: SELECT rows, then DELETE by their nonces, within a transaction.
- deleteMail(db, nonce: string): Promise<void>

Mail is end-to-end encrypted by the client — the server stores the ciphertext opaquely. The server never decrypts mail.

Zod schemas (src/mail/schemas.ts):
- MailSchema: { nonce, recipient, mailID, sender, header, cipher, group, extra, mailType, time, forward, authorID, readerID }
- SendMailBodySchema: z.object({ nonce, recipient, mailID, sender, header, cipher, group?, extra?, mailType, forward?, authorID, readerID })
title: Implement mail system

close_reason: Implemented: challenge-response auth (NaCl), 2048-byte limit, 5s ping heartbeat, deviceID registry, onMail dispatch, powerLevels.ts. 109/109 tests green (c5df824).
description:
Implement the WebSocket connection manager (src/ws/ClientManager.ts) using ws directly (no express-ws).

Responsibilities:
- Attach ws.Server to the existing Express http.Server
- Challenge-response authentication flow:
  1. Client connects → server sends random 32-byte challenge
  2. Client signs challenge with their device signing key (NaCl/tweetnacl)
  3. Server verifies signature against stored device signKey
  4. On success, client is registered as authenticated
- Message format: 32-byte header + variable MessagePack body (@msgpack/msgpack). Max total size: 2048 bytes. Hard-reject larger messages.
- Ping/pong keep-alive: 5-second interval, disconnect unresponsive clients
- Device-specific routing: each authenticated connection keyed by deviceID
- Resource dispatch: parse message header to route to correct handler (initially: 'mail' resource type)
- Emit events (EventEmitter) when messages arrive so Spire class can forward to recipients

Power level constants (src/ws/powerLevels.ts):
  INVITE = 25, CREATE = 50, DELETE = 50, EMOJI = 25

Uses: tweetnacl for signature verification, @msgpack/msgpack for encoding/decoding
title: Implement WebSocket connection manager

close_reason: Device management implemented: createDevice, retrieveDevice, retrieveDeviceBySignKey, retrieveUserDeviceList, deleteDevice, markDeviceLogin. schemas.ts with Zod schemas and inferred types.
description:
Implement device management in src/devices/index.ts.

Privacy model: NaCl signature verification is the route handler's responsibility. Service functions receive already-verified data and perform DB operations only.

Functions to implement:

createDevice(db, owner: string, payload: DevicePayload): Promise<Device>
  payload: { signKey: string (hex 64 chars), preKey: string (hex), preKeySignature: string (hex), preKeyIndex: number, deviceName: string }
  - Validates signKey: must match /^[0-9a-f]{64}$/
  - Validates owner: must be valid UUID
  - Generates deviceID = uuid.v4()
  - Atomically inserts: devices row + preKeys row
  - Throws on duplicate signKey (UNIQUE constraint)

retrieveDevice(db, identifier: string): Promise<Device | null>
  - If identifier is a UUID: query by deviceID WHERE deleted = 0
  - If identifier is 64 hex chars: query by signKey WHERE deleted = 0
  - Returns null for deleted devices or unknown identifiers

retrieveUserDeviceList(db, userID: string): Promise<Device[]>
  - Returns only devices WHERE owner = userID AND deleted = 0

deleteDevice(db, deviceID: string): Promise<void>
  - Soft delete: UPDATE devices SET deleted = 1 WHERE deviceID = ?
  - Also deletes associated preKeys and oneTimeKeys rows

markDeviceLogin(db, deviceID: string): Promise<void>
  - UPDATE devices SET lastLogin = now() WHERE deviceID = ?

Zod schemas (src/devices/schemas.ts):
  - DevicePayloadSchema: { signKey, preKey, preKeySignature, preKeyIndex, deviceName }
  - DeviceSchema: { deviceID, signKey, owner, name, lastLogin, deleted }
title: Implement device management

close_reason: Auth tests written and committed in a51fef8. Covers hex utils, verifyNaClSignature, createTokenStore, hashPassword/verifyPassword, registerUser, loginUser. Tests fail on stubs as expected — implementation is vex-chat-xyx.
description:
Write vitest tests defining expected authentication behavior — full fidelity with upstream NaCl privacy model.

Core privacy model (must be reflected in tests):
- User identity is cryptographically bound to a client-generated NaCl Ed25519 key pair
- userID = uuid.stringify(nacl.sign.open(signed, signKey)) — derived from client crypto, never server-assigned
- userID equals the registration token UUID, proved via device key signature
- Action tokens are single-use in-memory UUIDs (not JWTs), consumed on first use
- Token stores are isolated per server instance (createTokenStore() factory)
- Server cannot forge user identities or actions

Test coverage (all implemented in src/auth/__tests__/auth.test.ts):

hex utilities:
- encodeHex produces lowercase hex string
- decodeHex round-trips through encodeHex

verifyNaClSignature:
- Returns original message for a valid Ed25519 signature
- Returns null for signature from wrong key
- Returns null for tampered signed message

createTokenStore (in-memory single-use token store):
- Creates token with correct scope and UUID key
- validate() returns true once for correct scope
- Single-use: second validation of same token fails
- Rejects wrong scope
- Rejects unknown key
- Store instances are isolated
- Supports all 7 token types: file, avatar, register, device, invite, emoji, connect

hashPassword / verifyPassword (PBKDF2-SHA512, 1000 iterations, 32-byte output):
- Returns hex-encoded hash (64 chars) and salt
- Same password → different hashes (random salt)
- verifyPassword true for correct, false for wrong

registerUser(db, regKey, payload):
- userID = uuid.stringify(regKey) = original registration token UUID
- Creates user + device + preKey atomically
- Password stored as hash+salt, not plaintext
- Rejects duplicate username
- Rejects duplicate signKey
- Rejects username not matching /^\w{3,19}$/

loginUser(db, username, password):
- Returns JWT on correct credentials
- JWT payload is censoredUser: { user: { userID, username, lastSeen } } — no passwordHash/Salt
- JWT expires in 7 days
- Returns null for wrong password or unknown username

Key packages: tweetnacl, uuid (parse/stringify), jose (decodeJwt for test assertions)
title: [TEST] User authentication tests

close_reason: Closed
description:
Define the full database schema using Kysely's built-in migration system. Must support both SQLite (better-sqlite3 dialect) and PostgreSQL (pg dialect) via DB_TYPE env var.

Tables to define (11 total):
1. users — userID (uuid PK), username (unique), passwordHash, passwordSalt, lastSeen
2. devices — deviceID (uuid PK), signKey (unique), owner (FK → users), name, lastLogin, deleted (bool)
3. preKeys — keyID (uuid PK), userID (idx), deviceID (unique+idx), publicKey, signature, index
4. oneTimeKeys — keyID (uuid PK), userID (idx), deviceID (idx), publicKey, signature, index
5. mail — nonce (uuid PK), recipient (idx), mailID, sender, header, cipher (text), group, extra, mailType, time, forward, authorID, readerID
6. servers — serverID (uuid PK), name, icon
7. channels — channelID (uuid PK), serverID, name
8. permissions — permissionID (uuid PK), userID (idx), resourceType, resourceID (idx), powerLevel (int)
9. files — fileID (uuid PK), owner (idx), nonce
10. emojis — emojiID (uuid PK), owner (idx), name
11. invites — inviteID (uuid PK), serverID (idx), owner, expiration

Kysely setup:
- Define Database interface (src/db/types.ts) mapping table names to row types — this is the source of truth for all table types
- Create Kysely instance factory (src/db/index.ts) that reads DB_TYPE and returns a typed Kysely<Database>
- SQLite: use better-sqlite3 dialect (kysely + better-sqlite3)
- Postgres: use built-in Kysely pg dialect
- Migrations in src/db/migrations/ using Kysely Migrator

Note: Zod schemas for each domain's row types belong in the domain files, not here. This task is purely schema/migration.
title: Implement DB schema (Kysely migrations — 11 tables, SQLite + Postgres)

close_reason: Switched to argon2id (m=19MiB, t=2, p=1) in cf4b612. PBKDF2 1000-iter removed. argon2 added as dependency. Auth tests updated for PHC format.
description:
Current implementation uses PBKDF2-SHA512 with 1000 iterations, matching the original vex-chat/spire spec. OWASP 2025 minimum for PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA512 is 210,000 iterations. At 1000 iterations, a modern GPU can brute-force the entire space of common passwords in seconds.

Password hashing is purely server-side and not part of the E2E protocol — clients just send the password over TLS, the server hashes it. Increasing iterations does NOT break any client compatibility.

Options:
1. Increase PBKDF2 iterations to 210,000 (minimum OWASP compliant)
2. Switch to argon2id (OWASP preferred: m=19MiB, t=2, p=1) using the 'argon2' npm package
3. Use Node.js native crypto.scrypt() as a zero-dep memory-hard alternative

Recommendation: argon2id is the OWASP preferred choice for new projects. If keeping PBKDF2 (e.g. for FIPS compliance), minimum 210,000 iterations.

Blocks: any public deployment of the server.
title: SECURITY: PBKDF2 iterations 1000 is 210x below OWASP minimum (210,000)

close_reason: Fixed in dd52884: changed signed[70] to signed[5] — 70 was out of bounds for a 3-byte message (signed.length=67), so tampering was a no-op.
description:
In src/auth/__tests__/auth.test.ts, the 'returns null for a tampered signed message' test does:

  const signed = nacl.sign(new Uint8Array([1, 2, 3]), kp.secretKey)
  signed[70]\! ^= 0xff

nacl.sign produces a 67-byte array (64-byte sig + 3-byte msg). Index 70 is out of bounds. Uint8Array silently ignores out-of-bounds writes, so the tampering is a no-op, the signature remains valid, and nacl.sign.open returns the message instead of null.

Fix: change the tamper index to something in-bounds, e.g. signed[5]\! ^= 0xff (within the 64-byte signature portion) so the signature is actually corrupted.
title: Fix tampered-message test: signed[70] is out of bounds for a 3-byte message

close_reason: All 29 HTTP integration tests passing (215 total). Root cause was JWTPayloadSchema re-exported but not imported into scope in auth.service.ts — verifyJWT silently returned null on every call.
description:
Implement all Express HTTP route handlers for apps/spire using express@5. Every route file must also call registry.registerPath() to register its routes into the OpenAPI registry (from src/openapi.ts).

## Architecture requirements (must follow docs/architecture.md)

### Middleware order in buildApp()
1. helmet() — security headers, must be first
2. cors() — CORS headers
3. express.json() — body parsing (+ @msgpack/msgpack up to 20MB)
4. globalRateLimit — express-rate-limit for all routes
5. pino-http — request logging with IP/UA redacted (see below)
6. feature routers — createAuthRouter(), createUserRouter(), etc.
7. 404 handler — catch-all
8. errorMiddleware — MUST be last, MUST have exactly 4 params (err, req, res, next)

### Validation middleware (src/middleware/validate.ts)
Implement validateBody(schema) and validateParams(schema) factory functions. Route handlers receive typed, already-validated req.body — they never call .parse() themselves.

### Typed request properties (src/types/express.d.ts)
Declare global Express namespace extension so req.user and req.device are typed everywhere. Never cast req inside handlers.

### Router factory pattern
Each domain gets a factory: createAuthRouter(db, tokenStore), createUserRouter(db), etc. Auth endpoints get a tighter rate limiter applied at the router level (not app level).

### Thin controllers
Route handlers must be under 15 lines. One service call per handler. No business logic in handlers.

## Middleware specifics
- checkAuth: validates JWT from Authorization: Bearer header. Uses Zod to parse JWT payload. Attaches typed user to req.user.
- checkDevice: validates device action token. Attaches typed device to req.device.
- pino-http: MUST use serializers to suppress req.remoteAddress, req.headers['user-agent'], res.remoteAddress. Hard privacy requirement.
- Security: helmet, cors (with credentials), cookie-parser
- Scalar docs: mount @scalar/express-api-reference at GET /docs (theme: 'purple')
- Raw spec: GET /openapi.json returning generateOpenAPIDocument()

## Route groups (each in src/routes/)
- auth.ts: POST /auth, POST /register, POST /whoami, POST /goodbye, GET|POST /token/:tokenType
- users.ts: GET /user/:id, GET /user/:id/devices, GET /user/:id/permissions, GET /user/:id/servers, DELETE /user/:userID/devices/:deviceID
- devices.ts: POST /user/:id/devices, GET /device/:id, POST /device/:id/keyBundle, POST /device/:id/mail, POST /device/:id/connect, GET /device/:id/otk/count, POST /device/:id/otk
- servers.ts: GET /server/:id, POST /server/:name, DELETE /server/:id, POST /server/:id/channels, GET /server/:id/channels, DELETE /channel/:id, GET /server/:serverID/permissions, DELETE /permission/:id, GET /server/:serverID/invites, POST /server/:serverID/invites
- files.ts: GET /file/:id, GET /file/:id/details, POST /file/, POST /file/json
- avatars.ts: GET /avatar/:userID, POST /avatar/:userID, POST /avatar/:userID/json
- emoji.ts: GET /emoji/:emojiID, GET /emoji/:emojiID/details, POST /emoji/:serverID, POST /emoji/:serverID/json
- groups.ts: POST /userList/:channelID

## Route file pattern
1. Define Zod request/response schemas with .openapi() annotations
2. registry.registerPath() for each route
3. Express handler using validateBody/validateParams + one service call

## File limits
- emoji: 256KB
- files/avatars: 20MB
- Allowed image types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/apng, image/avif

## Token types
file, avatar, register, device, invite, emoji, connect (10-minute expiry each)

## Optional (dev only)
express-openapi-validator with validateRequests: true, validateResponses: true
title: Implement HTTP API routes and middleware

close_reason: 16 TDD tests: server CRUD (6), channel CRUD (7), getUserServers (3 — found/empty/scoped). All red — committed b69d79e.
description:
Write vitest tests for server and channel CRUD before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- createServer(name, icon) creates a server and returns it
- getServer(serverID) returns server or null for unknown ID
- getServers() returns all servers
- deleteServer(serverID) removes the server
- createChannel(serverID, name) creates a channel linked to server
- getChannel(channelID) returns channel or null
- getChannels(serverID) returns all channels for that server
- deleteChannel(channelID) removes the channel
- getUserServers(userID) returns all servers the user has a permission entry for
title: [TEST] Server & channel management tests

close_reason: 9 TDD tests written: saveMail (3), retrieveMail (5 — including relay delete), deleteMail (2). All red — committed 7c805e9.
description:
Write vitest tests for encrypted mail before implementation.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):
- saveMail(data) inserts a mail row with all required fields
- retrieveMail(deviceID) returns all mail for that device
- retrieveMail returns empty array when no mail
- retrieveMail DELETES the returned mail rows after returning them (privacy requirement: mail is a relay, not a store — messages are deleted from the server after the recipient fetches them)
- After a successful retrieveMail, calling retrieveMail again returns empty array (mail is gone)
- deleteMail(nonce) removes a specific mail row by nonce (for explicit deletion paths)
- Mail nonce must be unique (duplicate nonce is rejected)
- retrieveMail only returns mail for the specified recipient device, not others
- saveMail with missing required fields throws

Privacy note: The server stores ciphertext only. Mail is deleted on retrieval — the server is a relay, not a store. See AGENTS.md.
title: [TEST] Mail system tests

close_reason: Implemented logger, uuid, config, openapi registry. Research-backed decisions documented. 79/79 tests pass.
description:
Implement three foundational utilities for apps/spire/src/utils/ AND the OpenAPI registry.

src/utils/logger.ts
- createLogger(service: string) factory using pino@9
- Pretty output in development (pino-pretty), JSON in production
- Binds service name as default metadata field

src/utils/uuid.ts
- createUUID(): string — wraps crypto.randomUUID() (Node 20+ native)
- uuidToUint8(uuid: string): Uint8Array — needed for WebSocket challenge/response

src/config.ts (top-level config module)
- Zod schema for all environment variables:
  - DB_TYPE: z.enum(['sqlite', 'postgres'])
  - DATABASE_URL: z.string() (postgres connection string, required if DB_TYPE=postgres)
  - SQLITE_PATH: z.string().optional() (defaults to './spire.db' if DB_TYPE=sqlite)
  - SPK: z.string() — NaCl server signing private key as hex. Used for NaCl crypto ops ONLY. NOT used as JWT secret.
  - JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32) — dedicated JWT HMAC secret. Must be at least 32 chars. Separate from SPK (key hygiene).
  - API_PORT: z.coerce.number().default(16777)
  - LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['trace','debug','info','warn','error']).default('info')
  - NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development','production','test']).default('development')
- Export parsed config object — throws with clear Zod error if any required var is missing
- Replaces loadEnv.ts — no process.exit(), throw instead

src/openapi.ts (OpenAPI registry — part of this task)
- Call extendZodWithOpenApi(z) once (this module is the single place this call lives)
- Create and export a shared OpenAPIRegistry instance
- Export generateOpenAPIDocument(): OpenAPIObject that calls OpenApiGeneratorV31 with:
  - openapi: '3.1.0'
  - info: { title: 'vex-chat spire', version: '0.1.0' }
  - servers: [{ url: 'http://localhost:{port}', variables: { port: { default: '16777' } } }]
- Export bearerAuth security scheme component registered on the registry

scripts/generate-openapi.ts (generation script — part of this task)
- Imports all route files (to trigger registry.registerPath() calls)
- Calls generateOpenAPIDocument()
- Writes openapi.json to project root with JSON.stringify(doc, null, 2)
- Add 'generate:openapi' and 'lint:openapi' scripts to package.json:
  - 'generate:openapi': 'node --import tsx/esm scripts/generate-openapi.ts'
  - 'lint:openapi': 'spectral lint openapi.json --ruleset .spectral.yaml'
title: Implement utility functions (logger, UUID, config/env via Zod)

close_reason: Removed DevicePayloadSchema.parse() from createDevice(). Removed service-layer signKey validation tests. 61/61 tests passing.
description:
Currently createDevice() calls DevicePayloadSchema.parse(payload) inside the service function. Per the layered architecture, validation belongs at the route/controller boundary — service functions should trust their typed inputs.

The service-layer parse() was added as a temporary measure while routes don't exist yet. When vex-chat-ekb implements routes, each route handler should:
1. Parse and validate the request body with the domain Zod schema
2. Return 400/422 on ZodError before calling the service
3. Pass already-validated typed data to the service function

At that point, the DevicePayloadSchema.parse() call inside createDevice() should be removed (or kept as an explicit internal assertion, not the primary validation boundary).

This applies to all future service functions — don't add schema.parse() calls inside them. Validate at the route boundary.
title: Move input validation to route layer, not service layer

close_reason: Closed
description:
Write vitest tests for the Kysely schema migrations before any migrations are implemented.

Tests to cover:
- Running migrations up on a fresh in-memory SQLite DB creates all 11 tables with correct columns
- Running migrations down cleanly removes all tables
- Column types and constraints are correct (uniqueness on username, signKey; foreign key owner → users)
- Idempotency: running migrations twice does not error
- Verify indexes exist on: userID in preKeys/oneTimeKeys, recipient in mail, serverID in channels, userID+resourceID in permissions, owner in files/emojis, serverID in invites

Setup: use better-sqlite3 in-memory mode (':memory:') so tests are fast and isolated. Each test gets a fresh DB instance.
title: [TEST] DB schema migration tests

close_reason: 29 red HTTP integration tests across all route groups. app.ts factory stub created. Committed 6b6a6c1.
description:
Write vitest integration tests for all HTTP routes before implementing them.

Use supertest + a real express app wired to an in-memory SQLite Kysely instance.

Test groups:
- Auth routes: POST /register creates user + returns JWT cookie; POST /auth returns JWT on valid creds, 401 on invalid; POST /whoami returns current user; POST /goodbye clears cookie
- Token routes: GET /token/:type requires auth, returns scoped token; invalid types rejected
- User routes: GET /user/:id returns censored user (no passwordHash); GET /user/:id/devices returns device list
- Device routes: POST /user/:id/devices registers new device; GET /device/:id/otk/count returns count; POST /device/:id/otk saves OTKs
- Server routes: POST /server/:name creates server (requires auth); GET /server/:id returns server; DELETE /server/:id requires DELETE power level
- Channel routes: POST /server/:id/channels creates channel; GET /server/:id/channels lists channels
- Permission routes: GET /server/:serverID/permissions lists perms; DELETE /permission/:id requires DELETE power
- Invite routes: POST /server/:serverID/invites requires INVITE power; GET lists invites
- File/avatar/emoji routes: basic upload + serve roundtrip
- Auth guards: all protected routes return 401 without valid JWT
title: [TEST] HTTP API integration tests

- getPermissions(userID) returns all permissions for a user
- getPermissionsByResource(resourceID) returns all permissions for a resource

close_reason: TDD tests written for all 6 key management functions (savePreKey, getPreKey, saveOTKs, consumeOTK, getOTKCount, getKeyBundle). 18 tests red — committed f7701f3.
description:
Write vitest tests for pre-key and one-time-key management before implementation.

Privacy model: pre-keys and one-time keys are NaCl Ed25519 public keys. Signatures are verified by route handlers before keys are stored. Service functions receive hex-encoded public keys and signatures.

Test cases (in-memory SQLite via Kysely):

preKeys (medium-term keys, one per device):
- savePreKey(db, userID, deviceID, publicKey, signature, index) stores a preKey row
- publicKey must be 64 hex chars — reject invalid format
- getPreKeys(db, deviceID) returns the preKey for that device
- Updating a preKey (saving new one with same deviceID) replaces old (deviceID is UNIQUE)

oneTimeKeys (single-use keys, consumed during key exchange):
- saveOTK(db, userID, deviceID, otks[]) stores multiple OTKs
- getOTK(db, deviceID) returns exactly one OTK ordered by index and atomically deletes it
- getOTK returns null if no OTKs remain (graceful — not an error)
- getOTKCount(db, deviceID) returns count of remaining OTKs
- After getOTK, count decrements by 1 (key is consumed)

key bundle (assembled for X3DH key exchange):
- getKeyBundle(db, deviceID) returns { signKey (device public key), preKey, otk }
- OTK is consumed as part of getKeyBundle retrieval
- getKeyBundle returns null if device not found
- otk field is null (not error) if no OTKs remain — preKey is always required
title: [TEST] Cryptographic key management tests

close_reason: Implemented in dd52884: decodeHex, encodeHex, verifyNaClSignature, createTokenStore (TTL sweep+unref), hashPassword, verifyPassword, registerUser, loginUser. All 33 auth tests pass.
description:
Implement user authentication in src/auth/index.ts — full fidelity with upstream NaCl privacy model.

Functions to implement:

decodeHex(hex: string): Uint8Array
encodeHex(bytes: Uint8Array): string
  — hex encoding utilities used throughout crypto operations

verifyNaClSignature(signedMessage: Uint8Array, publicKey: Uint8Array): Uint8Array | null
  — thin wrapper around nacl.sign.open(); exported so route handlers can use without importing tweetnacl directly

createTokenStore(): ITokenStore
  — factory returning an isolated in-memory single-use token store
  — store.create(scope): IActionToken → generates uuid.v4() token, stores { key, scope, time }
  — store.validate(key, scope): boolean → checks key exists, scope matches, age < 10 min, then deletes (single-use)
  — expiry: TOKEN_EXPIRY = 10 * 60 * 1000 ms

hashPassword(password: string): Promise<PasswordHash>
  — PBKDF2-SHA512, 1000 iterations, 32-byte output, random salt via crypto.randomBytes
  — returns { hash: hexString, salt: hexString }

verifyPassword(password, hash, salt): Promise<boolean>
  — regenerates PBKDF2 hash with stored salt, constant-time comparison

registerUser(db, regKey: Uint8Array, payload: RegistrationPayload): Promise<AuthUser>
  — validates username against /^\w{3,19}$/
  — userID = uuid.stringify(regKey) — client-derived, not server-assigned
  — hashes password with hashPassword()
  — atomically inserts: users row + devices row + preKeys row
  — throws on duplicate username (UNIQUE constraint) or duplicate signKey

loginUser(db, username, password): Promise<string | null>
  — queries users by username
  — verifies password with verifyPassword()
  — on success: signs JWT with jose SignJWT, payload { user: censorUser(row) }, expiresIn '7d'
  — censorUser strips passwordHash/passwordSalt, returns { userID, username, lastSeen }
  — returns null (not throw) for wrong password or unknown username

JWT: signed with process.env.JWT_SECRET (dedicated secret, not the NaCl SPK)
NaCl: use tweetnacl directly
Zod schemas (src/auth/schemas.ts):
  - RegistrationPayloadSchema
  - LoginBodySchema: { username, password }
  - CensoredUserSchema: { userID, username, lastSeen }
  - JWTPayloadSchema: { user: CensoredUserSchema, iat, exp }
title: Implement user authentication

description:
Reimplement the vex-chat/spire chat server from scratch in apps/spire. The goal is NOT a line-for-line port — it's a clean reimplementation of the core protocol logic using modern tooling.

Reference: github.com/vex-chat/spire (for understanding the protocol/behavior)
Target: apps/spire in this pnpm monorepo (TypeScript, ESM, Node 20+)

Tech choices:
- Kysely: type-safe SQL query builder (replaces Knex). Kysely Database interface drives all table types.
- Zod: schema and type validation for env vars, request bodies, and API I/O
- SQLite (better-sqlite3) or PostgreSQL (pg) depending on DB_TYPE env var — no MySQL
- ws: WebSocket server (no express-ws)
- express@5: HTTP server
- pino: logging
- tweetnacl: NaCl signing for WebSocket challenge-response auth
- pbkdf2: password hashing (SHA-512, 1000 iterations, per-user salt)
- jsonwebtoken: JWT auth (7-day expiry, httpOnly cookies)
- @msgpack/msgpack: message serialization (replaces msgpack-lite)
- vitest: testing (in-memory SQLite for test isolation)

Core domains to implement (each with tests-first):
1. DB schema (Kysely migrations, 11 tables)
2. Utility functions (logger, UUID, env/config via Zod)
3. User authentication (register, login, JWT, scoped tokens)
4. Device management (register, revoke, list)
5. Cryptographic key management (pre-keys, OTKs, key bundles)
6. Mail system (encrypted mail between devices)
7. Server & channel management
8. Permission/RBAC
9. File, avatar & emoji storage
10. Invite system
11. WebSocket connection manager
12. HTTP API routes
13. Application entry point
title: [EPIC] Reimplement vex-chat/spire core logic in apps/spire
